name: Session Flow Timing
summary: Trade repeatable opening-drive, sweep, or reversion behavior inside a specific intraday session window.
marketConditions: Session timing creates repeatable expansion or reversion behavior.
Liquidity profile matches the setup: open drive, lunch drift, or Asia range.
Entry only happens inside the defined time window.

context: Define exact clock windows before the session begins.
Map Asia high and low, London open, New York open, and prior session extremes.
Decide whether the window is for breakout or mean reversion before price arrives.
setup: Price reaches the level or range expected for that session window.
Liquidity sweep or opening drive forms at the planned time.
Session behavior matches the historical pattern you trade.
trigger: Break of opening range for expansion setups.
Reclaim of swept level for reversal setups.
Enter only inside the active session window, never after it is gone.
confirmation: Volume and range pick up during the planned window.
Price respects the session level after trigger.
No conflicting macro release is seconds away.
invalidation: Price accepts on the wrong side of the key session level.
Opening range direction flips and holds against the idea.
noTrade: You missed the planned time window.
The setup depends on news randomness instead of repeatable session behavior.
Spread and slippage are too high around the session open.

stopLoss: Hide stop beyond the session level or sweep extreme.
Use tighter stops for open-drive setups and wider structure stops for sweep reclaims.
Risk must fit session volatility, not yesterday's.
takeProfit: Target the opposite side of the opening range, ADR objective, or prior session high or low.
Take partials quickly on time-sensitive setups.
Keep runners only while session momentum remains intact.
management: Move to break-even after the opening impulse confirms.
Scale down size as the session edge decays.
Do not let a session scalp drift into a random hold.
timeExit: Exit by the end of the planned session window.
Flatten before lunch or rollover if the setup was open-drive based.
Close before liquidity dies if price stalls.
earlyExit: Session level fails immediately after entry.
Price cannot hold above or below the opening range.
Time window expires with no continuation.

sizing: Size smaller around the most volatile minutes of the open.
Only increase size when the session pattern is exceptionally clean.
No revenge trades after missing the primary session move.
exposureLimits: One active session thesis per instrument.
Stop after two failed session trades in the same block.
No new entries once the session edge has clearly expired.

journaling: Record the exact session and clock window traded.
Note whether the setup was breakout, sweep reclaim, or fade.
Review if you respected the time exit.
assistantPrompts: Which session windows are you trading and what exact clock times define them?
Is your edge an opening-drive breakout or a sweep-and-reclaim reversal?
What is your hard time exit if price stalls?
